title: NZ Holiday Parks — The Complete Family Guide (TOP 10 vs Freedom Camping)
meta_desc: Discover the best NZ holiday parks, compare TOP 10 parks with freedom camping options, and learn how to book
intro: With over 300 holiday parks across New Zealand, choosing the right one for your family can be overwhelming. TOP 10 holiday parks offer excellent facilities, but freedom camping can be a more budget-friendly option. In this guide, we'll explore the best NZ holiday parks, their facilities, and costs to help you plan your next family adventure.

sections.heading: TOP 10 Holiday Parks vs Freedom Camping
sections.body: TOP 10 holiday parks are a popular choice among families, offering a range of facilities such as swimming pools, playgrounds, and BBQ areas. However, they can be more expensive than freedom camping, with prices starting from around $40 per night for a powered site. Freedom camping, on the other hand, can be as cheap as $10 per night, but often lacks the facilities and amenities of a holiday park.

sections.heading: Booking Tips
sections.body: It's essential to book your holiday park or freedom camping site in advance, especially during peak season. You can book TOP 10 holiday parks online or through their app, while freedom camping sites can be booked through the Department of Conservation website or through a camping app like CamperMate. Prices start from around $40 per night for a powered site, but can be more expensive during peak season.

sections.heading: Best Holiday Parks by Region
list: North Island: TOP 10 Bay of Islands, TOP 10 Taupo, and TOP 10 Rotorua
South Island: TOP 10 Queenstown, TOP 10 Wanaka, and TOP 10 Franz Josef
Stewart Island: Oban Holiday Park and Stewart Island Backpackers

sections.heading: Get Ready for Your Next Family Adventure
